deck.title: GenAI
deck.tagline: Governance, execution, and scale — rendered from the shared deck manifest.
deck.introSubtitle: Manifest-driven deck rendering for the full case-study story

overview.title: Case Study Overview
overview.subtitle: How the AI-accelerated delivery story unfolds across governance, execution, and scale
overview.summary: This opener gives the audience the full story in one pass: the mission need, the platform model, the governance proof points, and the path to scale.

cards.title: Mission Need
cards.body: The agency needed one front door for fragmented IT demand.

cards.title: Operating Model
cards.body: The platform links discovery, approval, and procurement handoff.

cards.title: Human Governance
cards.body: Speed stayed credible because humans still owned review and release.

cards.title: What Follows
cards.body: The next pages prove governance, execution, and scale in detail.

talkingPoints: Lead with the mission problem before the technology.
Frame the platform as an operating model, not a catalog screen.
Use the rest of the deck as proof of governance and execution.
Keep the throughline on readiness, speed, and controlled handoff.
overview.callout: Use this slide as the executive opener, then move into the proof pages.

human.title: Human in the Loop
human.subtitle: AI accelerates delivery. Human judgment governs quality.
human.thesis: Accelerated by AI. Governed by human expertise.

cards.title: Governance First
cards.highlight: Approvals and legal clearance came before acceleration.
details: Risk, legal, and compliance teams approved AI use before production release.
Contract updates included explicit AI language and operating guardrails.

cards.title: Quality Gates
cards.highlight: Every AI-assisted line was reviewed before merge.
details: More time was invested in review discipline, not less.
Unit and integration testing validated output before release.
No merge moved forward until all gates passed.

cards.title: Context Engineering
cards.highlight: Standardized prompts, process learning sessions, and developer expertise provided the system the full context.
details: Architecture docs, data models, and coding standards were embedded in prompts.
Variance dropped because outputs stayed anchored to the real system.

leadershipPoints: Governance sat inside the workflow, not after the fact.
Humans kept release authority even when AI authored most of the implementation.
Review rigor made speed credible to leadership.

results.label: Reduction in development time
results.detail: through incorporation of AI tools in development processes

results.label: Of production code developed via AI

human.enablement: An internal hackathon built team fluency and confidence, turning experimentation into a repeatable delivery habit.
human.callout: AI generated code. Humans owned the decisions.

hurdles.title: Hurdles We Overcame
hurdles.subtitle: What changed from day one to delivery

cards.title: Prompt Standardization
cards.challenge: Ad-hoc prompts created uneven quality and rework.
cards.fix: Versioned prompt templates added consistent architecture context.

cards.title: Process Realignment
cards.challenge: Traditional reviews were not built for AI-assisted output.
cards.fix: AI-specific review checklists made quality gates explicit.

cards.title: Governance Clearance
cards.challenge: Policy approval was required before production use.
cards.fix: Risk and legal teams were engaged early to set guardrails.

cards.title: Team Enablement
cards.challenge: Skill levels with AI tools were uneven across the team.
cards.fix: A hands-on hackathon built shared confidence before delivery.

hurdles.callout: The early friction became the guardrails that let the team move fast later.

sprint.title: AI Sprint Cycle
sprint.subtitle: Human checkpoints at every stage of AI-assisted delivery
sprint.callout: The sprint changed, but human checkpoints stayed embedded throughout delivery.

future.title: Looking Ahead
future.subtitle: Better steering — not more automation — is the next multiplier

cards.title: Model Steering & Planning
cards.body: Guardrails move upstream into prompts, tools, and workflow defaults.

cards.title: Evolved Prompt Library
cards.body: Prompt assets become reusable, modular, and versioned.

cards.title: Human-Governed Pipeline
cards.body: Automation helps at every step, but humans still decide to merge and deploy.

cards.title: Team Enablement Kit
cards.body: New team members can learn the playbook in days, not months.

future.callout: The playbook is proven. The next gain comes from better steering, not less governance.

platform.title: Service Platform
platform.subtitle: One front door for IT demand, approvals, and procurement handoff
platform.summary: The platform creates one governed front door for IT demand, approvals, and procurement continuation, helping users get the right tools faster.

focusPanels.label: Overview
focusPanels.title: One governed entry point
focusPanels.body: Discovery, request intake, approvals, and procurement continuation all sit in one flow.

focusPanels.label: Capability Zoom
focusPanels.title: What users can do
focusPanels.body: Users can browse, research, track, and route requests without jumping tools.

focusPanels.label: Process Zoom
focusPanels.title: How requests move
focusPanels.body: Users request. Approvers review. Admins complete procurement in the right tool.

capabilities.title: Browse IT Goods
capabilities.body: See hardware and software options in one place.

capabilities.title: Research Solutions
capabilities.body: Explore available IT services before submitting demand.

capabilities.title: Track Requests
capabilities.body: Users and admins can see request status clearly.

capabilities.title: Continue Procurement
capabilities.body: Approved demand flows into the right downstream tool.

lanes.title: Review & Request Tool
lanes.subtitle: Service Platform
lanes.persona: User: Any authorized personnel with agency credentials
steps: Submit a request form routed to the right approver.
Approve or deny the queued request.

lanes.title: Procurement Tool
lanes.subtitle: e.g. Legacy Procurement
steps: Approved requests continue in the relevant procurement tool.

talkingPoints: Fast-changing IT demand needs one clear front door.
The platform simplifies discovery without weakening control.
Role-based routing keeps the right people in the loop.
The real payoff is faster access and stronger readiness.
platform.callout: This is not just a catalog. It is a governed path from need to procurement.
